barks.text: No... those two don't rhyme.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Almost. The string wants to be somewhere, just not there.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Grandpa always said a hunch is a fact you haven't earned yet. This one I haven't even met.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The string sags like it's embarrassed for me. Fair.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: LOCAL REPORTER CONNECTS TWO UNRELATED THINGS — town unimpressed, string exhausted.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Nope. Maybe after tea. Some strings only tie after tea.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: If that were true, half my other cards would be lying. Cards don't lie. Reporters just read them wrong.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. And the kettle whistled right when I tried it, which felt like an opinion.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: That's not a connection, that's two pins sharing a wall. Neighbors, not family.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Mm. If I still believe that in the morning, I'll owe the board an apology.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. That's me answering a question nobody asked — which is a hobby, not journalism.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Not yet. Some pairs are true and still not ready. This one's neither.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: That's generous thinking. Generous like putting a hat on a lamppost.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: I can hear Dot already: 'Sell me that in one sentence.' I could not sell that in a paragraph.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. And I'm starting to think the string budget should count against me personally.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: That's two left shoes, Gibbons. Technically a pair. Practically a limp.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. Even the divorced pigeons agree on more than these two.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Holding this theory feels like holding Gino's fruit through a whole interview. Heavy. Sticky. Not mine to keep.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: It's a kind thought. Kind and wrong can share a sentence; they can't share a string.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Nope. That's a first-draft connection. Warren rewrites joy three times; I can rewrite one string.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: If this were true, Poppy would have laminated it by now.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Locks are terrible gossips and even they haven't heard this one.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: That's half a sentence pretending to be a whole one. I've learned to wait for the wind to drop.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Otto would call that a thin pull. All water, no crema.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: As Gino would say: a tragedy. A small one. But a tragedy.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: This connection has jam on it. Somebody enthusiastic got here before the facts did.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Tuck tests pastry with more rigor than I just tested that idea.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. Journalism has ETHICS. I read about it. An eleven-year-old told me where.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Ivy holds what it grows over. This string holds nothing. Botany wins.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: If I filed that connection, Dot would return it with the red pen and a personal note. Possibly a bite.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. Though it would make a decent chalkboard special: THE LONG SHOT — mostly foam.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Grandpa filed from a rowboat once. Even mid-river, he'd have rowed away from this one.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: There's no contingency card for this connection. Not even PLAN F: SWANS covers it.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Long exposure, Gibbons: hold the shutter open long enough and the truth smears into view. This one's just smear.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: That theory covers everything and shows nothing. I know an umbrella like that.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The marrow died for less certainty than this, and the marrow still deserved better.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Evelyn would prune that connection at the stem. Cleanly. While maintaining eye contact.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: One (1) sherry of an idea. Margie would quote it back to me verbatim and it would not survive the quoting.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: This theory hums while it sneaks. I've seen how that ends: hole number six.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: A document, a witness, or my own eyes. This has none of the three. It barely has the string.
barks.speaker: gary
